Introduction

INF10024 Business Digitalisation



Mark Dale and Rohan Bennett
February 2023

Broadly, where does this unit fit?

Lots to cover (but, also over the next 3 years)!

Wher e  do  I   fi nd  t he  cont ent ?


Go to:
Canvas > Modules


Each week has a multiple components


All are available a-synchronous, some are presented live

Is there a prescribed textbook?


No


The slides, recordings and weekly content constitute the body of knowledge for IBIS

How are cl asses structured each week?


Lecture Recordings (1 hour in total, cut up)
            Off-campus
            Use this to get the ball rolling, view it when you like


Lab/Tutorial (2 hours)
            On-campus
            Attend one per week = 24 hours total
            We ’ l l   g o   t h ro u g h   w e e k l y   c o n t e n t ,   Q & A   a n d   a s s i g n me n t   s u p p o r t


Online Activities and Self Learning (~10 hours / week)
            Assignment work
            One reading per week
            Also discussion board, consultation times, and other resources

How wi l l  we communi cate?


Canvas announcements = one-way broadcasts for everyone; unit content and unit progress
updates


Discussion forum = peer-to-peer interaction;  moderation by staff; a place to discuss and unpack
ideas; resolve assignments issues locally


Consultation times = times for direct contact with staff on content and/or administrative issues


On campus sessions = a place to get support and advice on unit content and assignments


Emails  = tutors if you’re unable to get to the lab/tutorial class –       and all queries in the first
instance; then -   convenors if you have personal issues/challenges; 48 hour turnaround; no
answers after 18:00 weekdays or on weekends

How wi l l  you be assessed? How do you pass?


To           a     c     h    i     e   v     e           a           p     a     s     s           o     r           h    i     g     h    e     r           g     r   a     d     e           i     n          t     h    i     s           u    n    i     t           y     o     u          m    u    s     t           o     b     t     a     i     n          a           m    i     n    i     m    u    m     aggregate of all
available marks of at least 50%.

How do I i nterpret the assessment rubri cs?



         Each column
         repres en t s  a
         performanc
         e level



                Each row
                repres en t s
                assessment
                criteria

How does the gradi ng system work?


High Distinction            =         80 -100


Distinction                 =         70 -79


Credit                      =         60 -69


Pass                        =         50 –59


N                           =         less than 50


WD                          =         withdrew (before census date)

What   about   l at e  submi ssi ons  and  ext ensi ons?


Late submission penalty is 10% of the total value of the assessment per day


If 5 days or more late 100% deduction is applicable


Extensions are not normally given, unless there is a compelling case


In all cases documentation such as doctor  ’s certificate will be required

Does referenci ng and plagi ari sm matter?


Ye   s  !


Please refer to university policy re g ard i ng   re f e re nci ng   and   p l ag i ar i s m


Referencing is crucial     in any university-level assessments


Harvard Style is required for assignments in this unit (see ‘Harvard Style Guide’ PDF in Resources
Folder) … or any style that works, provided you are consistent within your work


For more support      please ask your tutor or librarian

Making a Start
Business Digitalisation

What   ar e  our   cor e  concept s?


Business    :: the trading of goods and services between parties for
the purposes of profit making                                                                        In BuDi we also include
                                                                                                     government, NFPs, and
                                                                                                              NGOs



Information :: what is conveyed by the sequencing of things
                                                                                                    In BuDi this manifests as
                                                                                                        data in databases,
                                                                                                     software, and networks


Systems    :: related entities working together to complete a task
within a bounded        environment                                                                   In BuDi these entities
                                                                                                    include people, process,
                                                                                                      data, technology, and
                                                                                                              rules

Why  does  busi ness  di gi t al i zat i on  oft en  fai l ?


IT Projects still fail…


Sometimes spectacularly …100s of millions of dollars


Professionally speaking
Large bank-    Enterprise architecture project (9 $billion) over 7 years
Large energy retailer –     data quality issues –    literally lost 3 oil rig platforms
Large bank -   mispend     ($300 mill annually over 10 or more years)
Large bank -   $250 mill spent and nothing operational to show for
Pharmaceutical -     $250 mill Datawarehouse re p l ace me nt   p roj e ct


….and, yet organisations must continue to invest in IT to compete and grow

Is there an even bigger concern for business?

What   does  t hi s  mean  for   CEOs  and  Di r ect or s?

Meanwhi l e,   what  about  the  bri ght  si de?


Digital technologies help us to do business cheaper, better and faster (sometimes)
           Digitalization of work (e.g. tertiary education)
           Increasing connectivity and integration (especially post-Covid19)
           Online and ease of service access (e.g. medical)
           Robotics and automation (e.g.
           Digital currencies and enabling Blockchain tech
           IoT


Conflation of digital technologies allows us to create totally new businesses
           Mobility services (e.g. ride-sharing platforms)
           Constructions (e.g. unitisation      of commercial construction)

Are technology companies now the main players?


Ye   s  ,       ‘ m  o v   e       o v   e   r       o  i   l   ’   …


                            The world’s largest                                                           US$30 Billion
                             accommodation                                                            Owns no real estate
                                  provider

                            The world’s largest                                                           US$52-68bn
                                taxi service                                                            Owns no vehicle


                             The world’s most                                                           US$250 Billion
                                  valuable                                                            Owns no inventory
                               marketplace

                             The world’s most                                                            US$350 Billion
                              popular media                                                            Owns no content
                                 company
